---
title: python basics
---

```{css css_custom, code=readLines("_css_custom.css"), echo=FALSE}
```
```{r knitr_config, code=readLines("_knitr_config.R"), echo=FALSE}
```

*********************************************************************

w3schools tutorials:

* https://www.w3schools.com/python/default.asp  
* https://www.w3schools.com/python/ref_string_format.asp  

datacamp tutorials:

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/setup-data-science-environment  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-developer-set-up  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/usage-asterisks-python  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/argument-parsing-in-python  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/data-structures-python  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-data-type-conversion  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/functions-python-tutorial  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/fuzzy-string-python  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/understanding-recursive-functions-python  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/role-underscore-python  

*********************************************************************

## background

* created by Guido van Rossum
* released in 1991

*********************************************************************

## installation

```{sh}
which python
python --version
```

```{r}
knitr::opts_chunk$get("engine.path")$python
```

*********************************************************************

## general

* variable/function names are `lower_case` and `separated_with_underscores`  
* named constants are in `ALL_CAPITAL_LETTERS`  
* classes are in `CamelCase`  

*********************************************************************

## variables

```{python}
x, y, z = "a", 1, 2.2
print(type(x))
print(type(y))
print(type(z))
```

### str

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-string-tutorial

Concatenate with `+`

```{python}
print("Orange" + "Banana")
```

Coerce/cast with `str()`

```{python}
print(str(x))
print(str(y))
print(str(z))
```

Create with `str()`

```{python}
print(str(1))
print(str(2.8))
print(str("3"))
```

Use `"""` or `'''` for multiline strings

```{python}
a = """Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet,
consectetur adipiscing elit,
sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt
ut labore et dolore magna aliqua."""
print(a)
```

Index with `[`

```{python}
a = "Hello, World!"
print(a[0])
print(a[0:5])
print(a[-1])
print(a[-6:])
```

Built-in functions

```{python}
a = "    Hello, World!    "
print(len(a))
```

Methods

```{python}
a = "    Hello, World!    "
print(a.lower())
print(a.upper())
print(a.strip())
print(a.replace("H", "J"))
print(a.split(","))
```

```{python}
name = "Darren"
age = "41"
txt = "Hello, my name is {} and I am {}"
txt.format(name, age)
```

### int

Coerce/cast with `int()`

```{python}
# print(int(x)) # throws and error
print(int(y))
print(int(z))
```

Create with `int()`

```{python}
print(int(1))
print(int(2.8))
print(int("3"))
```

### float

Coerce/cast with `float()`

```{python}
# print(float(x)) # throws and error
print(float(y))
print(float(z))
```

Create with `float()`

```{python}
print(float(1))
print(float(2.8))
print(float("3"))
```

*********************************************************************

## collections

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-list-index  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-tuples-tutorial  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/sets-in-python  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/18-most-common-python-list-questions-learn-python  

property                      | list  | tuple | set   | dict
:---------------------------- | ----- | ----- | ----- | -----
ordered                       | yes   | yes   |       | 
changeable                    | yes   |       | yes   | yes 
indexed                       | yes   | yes   |       | yes    
duplicates allowed            | yes   | yes   |       |     
constructor                   | `[]`  | `()`  | `{}`  | `{}`
                              |       |       |       | 
**method**                    | **list** | **tuple** | **set** | **dict**
`add`                         |       |       | yes   | 
`append`                      |  yes  |       |       |  
`clear`                       |  yes  |       | yes   | yes 
`copy`                        |  yes  |       | yes   | yes 
`count`                       |  yes  |  yes  |       |  
`difference`                  |       |       | yes   | 
`difference_update`           |       |       | yes   | 
`discard`                     |       |       | yes   | 
`extend`                      |  yes  |       |       |  
`fromkeys`                    |       |       |       | yes
`get`                         |       |       |       | yes
`index`                       |  yes  |  yes  |       |  
`insert`                      |  yes  |       |       |  
`intersection`                |       |       | yes   | 
`intersection_update`         |       |       | yes   | 
`isdispoint`                  |       |       | yes   | 
`issubset`                    |       |       | yes   | 
`issuperset`                  |       |       | yes   | 
`items`                       |       |       |       | yes
`keys`                        |       |       |       | yes
`pop`                         |  yes  |       | yes   | yes 
`popitems`                    |       |       |       | yes
`remove`                      |  yes  |       | yes   |  
`reverse`                     |  yes  |       |       |  
`setdefault`                  |       |       |       | yes
`sort`                        |  yes  |       |       |  
`symmetric_difference`        |       |       | yes   | 
`symmetric_difference_update` |       |       | yes   | 
`union`                       |       |       | yes   | 
`update`                      |       |       | yes   | yes
`values`                      |       |       |       | yes


Constructors:

```{python}
l = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
t = ("apple", "banana", "cherry")
s = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
d = {"apple" : "green", "banana" : "yellow", "cherry" : "red"}
```

Alternative constructors:

```{python}
l = list(("apple", "banana", "cherry"))
t = tuple(("apple", "banana", "cherry"))
s = set(("apple", "banana", "cherry"))
d = dict(apple = "green", banana = "yellow", cherry = "red")
```

Class:

```{python}
type(l)
type(t)
type(s)
type(d)
```

Built-in functions:

```{python}
len(l)
len(t)
len(s)
len(d)
```

Indexing:

```{python}
l[0]
t[0]
# s[0] # throws an error
# d[0] # throws an error
d["apple"]
```

Loops:

```{python}
for element in l: print(element)
for element in t: print(element)
for element in s: print(element)
for key in d: print(key) # print keys
for key in d: print(d[key]) # print values
for value in d.values(): print(value)  # print values
for key, value in d.items(): print(key, value) # print keys and values
```

Querying:
  
```{python}
if "banana" in l: print("I love bananas")
if "banana" in t: print("I love bananas")
if "banana" in s: print("I love bananas")
if "banana" in d: print("I love bananas")
```

Set methods:

```{python}
s.add("orange")
s.update(["mango", "grapes"])
print(s)
s.discard("pear") # no error
# s.remove("pear") # error
```

Dict methods:

```{python}
d["apple"]
d.get("model")
d["apple"] = "red"
d["pear"] = "green"
print(d)
d.popitem()
print(d)
```

Keywords:

```{python}
del l[0]
print(l)
# del t[0] # throws an error
# del s[0] # throws an error
# del d[0] # throws an error
#del d["cherry"]
#print(d)
```

```{python}
del l
del t
del s
del d
```

*********************************************************************

## comprehensions

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-list-comprehension  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-dictionary-comprehension  

*********************************************************************

## operators

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-operators-tutorial  

**Arithmetic**

`%` - modulus  
`**` - exponentiation  
`//` - floor division  

**Logical**

`x < 5 and x < 10`  
`x < 5 or x < 4`  
`not(x < 5 and x < 10)`  

**Identity**

`x is y`  
`x is not y`  

**Membership**

`x in y`  
`x not in y`  

**Bitwise**

`&` : binary AND  
`|` : binary OR  
`^` : binary XOR  
`~` : binary flip bits  
`<<` : binary left shift  
`>>` : binary right shift  

```{python}
a = 60        #  60 = 0011 1100 
b = 13        #  13 = 0000 1101 
print(a & b)  #  12 = 0000 1100 
print(a | b)  #  61 = 0011 1101
print(a ^ b)  #  49 = 0011 0001
print(~a)     # -61 = 1100 0011
print(a << 2) # 240 = 1111 0000
print(a >> 2) #  15 = 0000 1111
```

**Assignment**

`x += 3` is the same as `x = x + 3`	 
`x -= 3` is the same as `x = x - 3`  
`x *= 3` is the same as `x = x * 3`	 
`x /= 3` is the same as `x = x / 3`	 
`x %= 3` is the same as `x = x % 3`	 
`x //= 3` is the same as `x = x // 3`	 
`x **= 3` is the same as `x = x ** 3`	 
`x ^= 3` is the same as `x = x ^ 3`  
`x &= 3` is the same as `x = x & 3`	 
`x |= 3` is the same as `x = x | 3`	 

*********************************************************************

## reserved words

*********************************************************************

## if else

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-if-elif-else  

*********************************************************************

## functions

### range

*********************************************************************

## files

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/reading-writing-files-python

*********************************************************************

## pickling

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/pickle-python-tutorial  

*********************************************************************

## decorators

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/decorators-python

*********************************************************************

## iterators

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-iterator-tutorial

*********************************************************************

## running scripts from the command line

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/running-a-python-script
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/modules-in-python

Scripting - passing args via command line

*********************************************************************

## f-strings

```{python}
x = 10
y = 20
print(f"x is {x}, y is {y} and z is {2*y}")
print(f"this is \"quoted\"")
print(f"""this works too""")
print(f"{{this is inside literal braces}}")
```

* can use `f` or `F`  
* can wrap strings in triple quotes  
* can use expressions inside the curly braces  
* use backslash to print literal quotes  
* use double brace to print literal brace  

*********************************************************************

## envs

create a new virtual environment inside directory new_venv.

$ python -m venv new_venv

source new_venv/bin/activate

*********************************************************************

## dates and times

*********************************************************************

## file paths

```python
sys.path.append('{}/myproject/'.format(os.path.expanduser('~')))
from mysript import myfunction
```

*********************************************************************

## databases

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/sqlite-in-python  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/mysql-python  

*********************************************************************

## docstrings

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/docstrings-python  

```python
def square(value):
    """Return the square of a value"""
    new_value = value ** 2
    return new_value
```

*********************************************************************

## scope

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/scope-of-variables-python

* global - defines the main body of a script  
* local - defined inside a function  
* built-in - names in the pre-defined built-ins module  

### nonlocal

* used when defining a function that is nested inside another function
* allows the nested function to use/modify variables defined in the outer function

```{python}
def myfunc1():
    x = "John"
    def myfunc2():
        nonlocal x
        x = "hello"
    myfunc2() 
    return x

print(myfunc1())
```

*********************************************************************

## packing + unpacking 

e.g. tuples

```python
even_nums = (2, 4, 6)
a, b, c = even_nums 
```

### asterisks

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/usage-asterisks-python

unpacking
nums = [i for i in range(1, 6)] 
## a will be 1 and b will be a list containing remaining elements 
a, *b = nums 
print(a, b)
# here first element will assign to a and last element to c 
a, *b, c = (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) 
print(a, b, c)

Combining iterables
# combining a tuple, list, set nums = [1, 2, 3] nums2 = (4, 5, 6) nums3 = {7, 8, 9} ## we convert the combined elements into any iterable we want ## here i am converting into a list _list = [*nums, *nums2, *nums3] _tuple = (*nums, *nums2, *nums3) _set = {*nums, *nums2, *nums3} 

Nested unpacking
languages = ["Python", "HTML", "CSS", "JS"] ## unpacking [[first_letter, *remaining], *other] = languages 

unpacking an iterable
nums = [i for i in range(1, 6)] 
## passsing list using the * 
print(*nums)

Packing function arguments
E.g. capture arguments as a list
def average(*nums): 
return sum(nums) / len(nums) 
print(average(1, 2, 3, 4, 5))

capture key-value pair elements using **.
def _object(name, **properties):
 print(name, properties) 
_object("Car", color="Red", cost=999999, company="Ferrari") 

*********************************************************************

## loops

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/loops-python-tutorial  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/for-loops-in-python

can be used to iterate over a sequence of a list, string, tuple, set, array, data frame.
For loops use the range function which returns a new list with numbers of that specified range based on the length of the sequence.
you can also use the index of elements in the sequence to iterate
The for loops in Python are zero-indexed.

range always expects an integer value.

for i in range(10): 
    print (i+1)

for i in range(len(sequence)): 
    print (sequence[i])

Or define the starting point, the ending point and the increment

for i in range(1,20,2): 
    print (i)

columns = ['sepal_length','sepal_width','petal_length','petal_width'] for indices, row in iris.iterrows(): for column in columns: iris.at[indices,column] = row[column] + 2 iris.head()

Lambda functions
x = [20, 30, 40, 50, 60]
y = map(lambda x: x * 5,x)
list(y)

*********************************************************************

## exception handling

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/exception-handling-python

*********************************************************************

## libraries

### datetime

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/converting-strings-datetime-objects  

.strptime(X, "%Y%m%d")

### json

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/json-data-python  

### os

```{python}
import os
print(os.path.expanduser('~'))
```

### re

* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-regular-expression-tutorial  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/regular-expressions-clean-strings  

### sys

```python
import sys
import os
sys.path.append('{}/github/how2/'.format(os.path.expanduser('~')))
sys.argv[0]
sys.argv[1]
print(sys.prefix)
```

### warnings

```python
# Hide warnings if there are any
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
```


*********************************************************************

Built-in vars
Special vars
magic vars
Print doesn't print enclosing quotes for strings
Raw strings e.g. print(r'C:\some\name') 
Multi line strings e.g.
print("""Usage: thingy [OPTIONS] -h Display this usage message -H hostname Hostname to connect to """)
print("""\ Usage: thingy [OPTIONS] -h Display this usage message -H hostname Hostname to connect to """)
Repetition and concatenation e.g. 3 * 'un' + 'ium
Control-D to exit command line python?
Comprehensions
https://www.kaggle.com/benhamner/python-data-visualizations
LightGBM
CatBoost
Vowpal Wabbit
